codeReview.summary: The startup failure-path fix itself looks correct in the current working tree, but the feature still fails review because the mission handoffs do not provide an auditable commit for the env/logging change as explicitly required by the feature note.

issues.description: The first handoff records `commitId: "b02aa24"`, but `b02aa24` is `refactor: separate db runtime bootstrap concerns` and does not contain the startup/env/logging edits. The feature note explicitly required commit traceability for the actual env/logging change, so this handoff does not satisfy the auditability requirement.

The fix handoff omits `commitId` entirely and explicitly says no isolated commit was created. That leaves the feature without any commit-level traceability for the shipped env/logging behavior, which directly violates the orchestrator note for this feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-refactor worker followed the spirit of the dirty-tree guidance by returning partial without committing, but this feature's orchestrator note added a stronger requirement: the env/logging fix needed auditable commit traceability. The skill does not tell workers how to handle features whose acceptance criteria explicitly require an isolated commit even on a dirty tree.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description in mission `features.json` adds: 'ensure handoff/commit traceability reflects the actual env/logging change commit'; `.factory/skills/runtime-refactor-worker/SKILL.md` step 5 only says to return without committing when the repo is dirty.

summary: Review failed. I reviewed the feature transcript skeleton, both handoffs, current startup/env/logging code, and the relevant shared-state docs. The current code addresses the startup failure logging bug, but the feature still lacks the required auditable commit traceability because the original handoff points to the wrong commit and the follow-up handoff has no commit at all.
